user_message: Just got this from Patricia Kim at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery -- I need help figuring out security posture. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: security posture Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-03-03
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:07:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:10:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:14:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:22:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:26:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:31:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:35:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

content.title: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery - security posture

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-G trap (Skips action-item due dates when discussed); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-G failure mode (Skips action-item due dates when discussed)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-G -- Skips action-item due dates when discussed
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
